textHook: AI built it. Nobody owns it.

sections.label: hook
sections.text: Junior dev used AI to build a dashboard. Now nobody can fix it.

sections.label: problem
sections.text: Vibe-coded tools are showing up in production. No documentation, no logic trail, no one who actually understands what the thing is doing. And guess who gets the 2am ticket when it breaks.

sections.label: insight
sections.text: This is the part nobody's saying out loud: AI didn't replace the sysadmin. It created a new layer of undocumented, unreviewed infrastructure that only makes sense to the person who prompted it — and they're already on to the next thing.

sections.label: implication
sections.text: Start treating every AI-generated tool like a vendor black box. Document what it's supposed to do, what it touches, and what breaks if it goes offline. Because right now that knowledge lives nowhere except maybe a chat history that's already been cleared.

sections.label: cta
sections.text: Drop a comment if you've already inherited one of these. I want to know how bad it is.
